title: Present Simple - Do/Does
titleTr: Geniş Zaman - Olumlu, Olumsuz ve Soru
description: Talk about habits, likes, and daily actions. Use do/does for questions and negatives. Go shopping at the supermarket!
descriptionTr: Alışkanlıkları, beğenileri ve günlük eylemleri anlat. Soru ve olumsuz için do/does kullan. Markette alışveriş yap!

vocabulary.example: I like coffee in the morning.
vocabulary.extra: Do you like Turkish food?

vocabulary.example: I want a bottle of water, please.
vocabulary.extra: What do you want for dinner?

vocabulary.example: I need some milk. We don't have any.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Biraz süte ihtiyacım var. Hiç yok.
vocabulary.extra: Do you need help with the bags?
vocabulary.extraTr: Poşetler için yardıma ihtiyacın var mı?

vocabulary.example: I usually buy groceries on Saturday.
vocabulary.extra: She buys fresh bread every morning.

vocabulary.example: I eat breakfast at 8 o'clock.
vocabulary.extra: He eats a lot of fruit.

vocabulary.example: I drink two liters of water every day.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Her gün iki litre su içerim.
vocabulary.extra: She doesn't drink coffee. She prefers tea.

vocabulary.example: My wife cooks dinner every evening.
vocabulary.extra: I don't cook very often. I usually order food.
vocabulary.extraTr: Çok sık yemek pişirmem. Genelde yemek sipariş ederim.

vocabulary.example: This store sells fresh vegetables.
vocabulary.extra: He sells electronics online.

vocabulary.example: Do you pay by cash or card?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Nakit mi yoksa kartla mı ödüyorsun?
vocabulary.extra: I always pay with my credit card.

vocabulary.example: How much does this cost?
vocabulary.extra: These tomatoes cost 2 dollars per kilo.

vocabulary.example: We need two loaves of bread.
vocabulary.extra: I like whole wheat bread.

vocabulary.example: Do we have any milk in the fridge?
vocabulary.extra: He drinks milk every morning.

vocabulary.example: I eat two eggs for breakfast.
vocabulary.extra: A dozen eggs, please.

vocabulary.example: She eats fruit after every meal.
vocabulary.extra: Fresh fruit is good for your health.

vocabulary.example: I don't eat enough vegetables.
vocabulary.extra: The vegetable section is over there.

vocabulary.example: How much does the meat cost?
vocabulary.extra: I don't eat meat. I'm vegetarian.

vocabulary.example: We usually eat chicken twice a week.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Genelde haftada iki kez tavuk yeriz.
vocabulary.extra: I want a kilo of chicken breast, please.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bir kilo tavuk göğsü istiyorum, lütfen.

vocabulary.example: Do you eat rice every day?
vocabulary.extra: Rice goes well with chicken.

vocabulary.example: I don't use sugar in my tea.
vocabulary.extra: Too much sugar is bad for you.

vocabulary.example: Can I have a glass of water, please?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bir bardak su alabilir miyim, lütfen?
vocabulary.extra: I always carry a bottle of water.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yanımda her zaman bir şişe su taşırım.

vocabulary.example: I like orange juice for breakfast.
vocabulary.extra: Do you have any apple juice?

vocabulary.example: I love cheese on my toast.
vocabulary.extra: She buys white cheese every week.

vocabulary.example: Do you need a bag?
vocabulary.extra: I always bring my own bag to the store.
vocabulary.extraTr: Markete her zaman kendi poşetimi getiririm.

vocabulary.example: What is the price of this?
vocabulary.extra: The prices are really high this month.

vocabulary.example: This brand is cheap and good.
vocabulary.extra: Where can I find cheap groceries?

vocabulary.example: This cheese is too expensive!
vocabulary.extra: Organic food is always more expensive.
vocabulary.extraTr: Organik yiyecek her zaman daha pahalıdır.

vocabulary.example: I always buy fresh vegetables.
vocabulary.extra: This bread is very fresh. It's from this morning.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu ekmek çok taze. Bu sabahtan.

vocabulary.example: There is a grocery store near my house.
vocabulary.extra: The store closes at 10 PM.

vocabulary.example: I always make a shopping list before I go.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gitmeden önce her zaman alışveriş listesi yaparım.
vocabulary.extra: What's on the list today?

vocabulary.example: I prefer tea over coffee.
vocabulary.extra: She prefers online shopping.

grammar.title: Present Simple - Positive, Negative & Questions
grammar.titleTr: Geniş Zaman - Olumlu, Olumsuz ve Soru Cümleleri
grammar.explanation: Use Present Simple for habits, routines, facts, and things you like or dislike. For I/you/we/they, use the base verb. For he/she/it, add -s or -es. Use 'do/does' for questions and 'don't/doesn't' for negatives.
grammar.explanationTr: Geniş Zaman alışkanlıklar, rutinler, gerçekler ve sevdiğin/sevmediğin şeyler için kullanılır. I/you/we/they ile fiilin yalın hali kullanılır. He/she/it ile fiile -s veya -es eklenir. Sorularda 'do/does', olumsuzda 'don't/doesn't' kullanılır.
table: Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
Yes, he does. / No, he doesn't.
Yes, she does. / No, she doesn't.
Yes, it does. / No, it doesn't.
Yes, we do. / No, we don't.
Yes, they do. / No, they don't.

tips.en: He/She/It → add -s: like → likes, eat → eats, buy → buys
tips.tr: He/She/It ile fiile -s eklenir: like → likes, eat → eats, buy → buys
-s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o ile biten fiillere -es eklenir: go → goes, wash → washes

tips.en: consonant + y → drop y, add -ies: carry → carries, try → tries
tips.tr: Ünsüz + y ile bitenler: y düşer, -ies eklenir: carry → carries, try → tries

tips.en: Does + he/she/it → verb goes back to base: She likes → Does she like? (NOT: Does she likes?)
tips.tr: Does ile soru yaparken fiilin -s'i düşer: She likes → Does she like? (Does she likes? YANLIŞ!)

tips.en: Frequency words: always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never → before the verb
tips.tr: Sıklık zarfları fiilden ÖNCE gelir: I always buy, She never eats

tips.en: How much does this cost? = What's the price? = How much is this?
tips.tr: Fiyat sorma: How much does this cost? = What's the price? = How much is this?

reading.text:
Hi! My name is Ayşe. Every Saturday morning, I go to the big supermarket near my house. I always make a shopping list before I leave.

I usually buy fresh vegetables first — tomatoes, peppers, onions, and potatoes. My family eats a lot of salad, so I need fresh vegetables every week. I also buy fruit — mostly apples and oranges. My kids love bananas, so I always get some.

Then I go to the dairy section. I buy milk, eggs, and white cheese. We eat eggs every morning for breakfast. My husband drinks a lot of milk, so I buy two bottles every time.

I don't buy a lot of meat because it's very expensive these days. I usually buy chicken instead — it's cheaper and my family likes it. I cook chicken with rice twice a week.

I prefer to pay by card, but some small stores don't accept cards. The total usually costs around 800-900 liras. It sounds expensive, but it's enough food for the whole week.

My husband doesn't come to the store with me. He doesn't like shopping! He prefers to stay home and watch TV. But he always helps me carry the bags when I get home. That's nice of him!
reading.textTr:
Merhaba! Benim adım Ayşe. Her cumartesi sabahı evimin yakınındaki büyük markete giderim. Gitmeden önce her zaman bir alışveriş listesi yaparım.

Genelde önce taze sebze alırım — domates, biber, soğan ve patates. Ailem çok salata yer, bu yüzden her hafta taze sebzeye ihtiyacım var. Bir de meyve alırım — çoğunlukla elma ve portakal. Çocuklarım muzu çok sever, o yüzden hep alırım.

Sonra süt ürünleri reyonuna giderim. Süt, yumurta ve beyaz peynir alırım. Her sabah kahvaltıda yumurta yeriz. Kocam çok süt içer, bu yüzden her seferinde iki şişe alırım.

Çok et almam çünkü bu aralar çok pahalı. Genelde onun yerine tavuk alırım — daha ucuz ve ailem seviyor. Haftada iki kez tavuklu pilav yaparım.

Kartla ödemeyi tercih ederim ama bazı küçük marketler kart kabul etmez. Toplam genelde 800-900 lira civarı tutar. Pahalı gibi görünüyor ama bütün hafta yetecek kadar yiyecek.

Kocam benimle markete gelmez. Alışverişi sevmez! Evde kalıp TV izlemeyi tercih eder. Ama eve geldiğimde poşetleri taşımama her zaman yardım eder. Bu onun çok tatlı bir hareketi!

questions.question: When does Ayşe go to the supermarket?
Why doesn't Ayşe buy a lot of meat?
What does Ayşe cook twice a week?
questions.questionTr: Ayşe haftada iki kez ne pişirir?

questions.question: How does Ayşe prefer to pay?
Why doesn't her husband come to the store?

fillBlanks.sentence: She ___ fresh bread from the bakery.
We ___ eat meat. We are vegetarian.
He ___ like shopping. He stays home.
I always make a shopping ___ before I go.
This cheese is too ___! I can't buy it.

multipleChoice.question: "She ___ vegetables every week." Boşluğa ne gelir?
"___ he drink milk?" Boşluğa ne gelir?
"He go___ to work every day." Doğru ek hangisi?
"Does she like tea?" → Evet. Doğru kısa cevap?

sentenceOrder.correct: Does she prefer tea or coffee
I always make a shopping list

translation.english: I buy fresh bread every morning.
acceptAlso: I buy fresh bread every morning
Every morning I buy fresh bread

translation.english: She cooks chicken twice a week.
acceptAlso: He cooks chicken twice a week
She cooks chicken twice a week

dialog.title: At the Supermarket
dialog.context: Eyüp markete gidiyor. Kasiyer ve reyon görevlisi ile konuşuyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Excuse me, do you sell fresh chicken here?
lines.textTr: Affedersiniz, burada taze tavuk satıyor musunuz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Yes, we do! The chicken section is in aisle 3. Do you need anything else?
lines.textTr: Evet, satıyoruz! Tavuk reyonu 3. koridorda. Başka bir şeye ihtiyacınız var mı?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, I also need eggs and milk. Where do I find them?
lines.textTr: Evet, yumurta ve süte de ihtiyacım var. Onları nerede bulurum?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Eggs and milk are in the dairy section — aisle 5, on the right side.
lines.textTr: Yumurta ve süt, süt ürünleri reyonunda — 5. koridor, sağ taraf.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you! How much does the white cheese cost?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: The small one costs 80 liras and the big one costs 140 liras.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I'll take the big one. My family eats a lot of cheese!
lines.textTr: Büyüğünü alayım. Ailem çok peynir yiyor!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good afternoon! Do you need a bag?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: No, thanks. I always bring my own bag. How much is the total?
lines.textTr: Hayır, teşekkürler. Her zaman kendi poşetimi getiririm. Toplam ne kadar?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Let me see... Your total is 347 liras. Do you pay by cash or card?
lines.textTr: Bir bakayım... Toplamınız 347 lira. Nakit mi kartla mı ödeyeceksiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: By card, please. I don't usually carry cash.
lines.textTr: Kartla lütfen. Genelde yanımda nakit taşımam.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: No problem! Here's your receipt. Have a nice day!
lines.textTr: Sorun değil! Buyurun fişiniz. İyi günler!

questions.question: What does Eyüp need from aisle 3?
How much does the big white cheese cost?
Does Eyüp need a bag?
options: No, he brings his own bag
